title: dendrobium parvulum subsp. parvulum
intro: Dendrobium parvulum subsp. parvulum is a species of orchid native to tropical forests in Southeast Asia, specifically found in Indonesia, Malaysia, and the Philippines, known for its unique, small flowers. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dendrobium parvulum subsp. parvulum are said to include reducing fever, alleviating pain and swelling, and treating respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion by boiling the roots and leaves in water, or as a tincture by extracting the active compounds with a solvent.
